metadata.title: Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition

beats.narration_text: Shalom. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of argentine ant supercolony: genetic diversity as a phase transition. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of argentine ant supercolony: genetic diversity as a phase transition
output: ✓ Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition

beats.narration_text: Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Low genetic diversity collapses war-torn territories into one empire. Drag the Founder Count slider to see the phase transition.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: founder-slider, speed-slider, tau-slider. Verify: Low genetic diversity collapses war-torn territories into one empire. Drag the Founder Count slider to see the phase tra. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: foun

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from yt-ant-supercolony.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: argentine ant supercolony: genetic diversity as a phase transition. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 1 to 30. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 1 to 30

beats.narration_text: Low genetic diversity collapses war-torn territories into one empire. Drag the Founder Count slider to see the phase transition.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of argentine ant supercolony: genetic diversity as a phase transition. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Argentine Ant Supercolony: Genetic Diversity as a Phase Transition simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
